key_insight: A unified analytical model links loss diagnosis and rational design for photoelectrochemical cells by quantifying how light absorption, transport, recombination, and interfacial effects limit performance in both BIJ and SEJ architectures.
engineering_relevance: It gives engineers a practical way to fit experimental I-V curves, identify the dominant bottlenecks in real devices, and translate those bottlenecks into concrete optimization strategies and material-selection windows.
method: Builds a unified physically based loss framework for BIJ and SEJ photoelectrodes
Generates ideal/real I-V curves, efficiency maps, and Sankey energy-flow diagrams
Fits experimental data to decompose thermodynamic, optical, recombination, and interfacial losses
evidence_signal: The framework is validated against state-of-the-art literature across solar water splitting, CO2 reduction, NH3 synthesis, and solar redox flow batteries, and compared across absorber classes such as Si, perovskites, hematite, and BiVO4.
watch_item: Because the model relies on fitting experimental I-V data and consistent parameterization, its diagnostic accuracy may depend on data quality and how well real device physics match the model assumptions.
paper_title: From Loss Diagnosis to Rational Design: A Unified Analytical Model for Photoelectrochemical Cells
